industry.headline: T1 Energy to Acquire KORE Power in $32 Million Deal
industry.summary: T1 Energy, a solar and battery manufacturing company, has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire KORE Power, a battery energy company. The acquisition is valued at $32 million. This strategic move involves the integration of KORE Power into T1 Energy's existing operations within the solar and battery manufacturing sectors to expand their market presence.

The funding will support the development and implementation of these solar energy installations to expand the company's renewable energy portfolio within the United States.

industry.headline: Rajasthan RERC Classifies GST Cut as Change-in-Law Event
industry.summary: The Rajasthan Electricity Regulatory Commission (RERC) has ruled that the reduction in goods and services tax (GST) on renewable energy devices and parts will be treated as a change-in-law event. The GST rate was reduced from 12% to 5%. This ruling applies to power purchase agreements (PPAs) signed within the state of Rajasthan.

industry.headline: Tamil Nadu Mandates Must-Run Status for Renewable Energy Stations
industry.summary: The Tamil Nadu Electricity Regulatory Commission (TNERC) has issued the State Load Dispatch Center (SLDC) Functions and Accountability Regulations, 2026. These rules mandate that renewable energy generating stations, nuclear power stations, and run-of-river hydro stations be treated as must-run stations. They should not be curtailed except for essential grid security reasons under the new regulations.

learn.title: BIPV: Solar in Buildings
learn.intro: BIPV integrates solar panels into building materials like windows or roofs, generating power without sacrificing design. This approach reduces land use for solar farms while making structures energy-efficient. With rising urbanization, BIPV offers a scalable solution for cities to meet clean energy targets, aligning with global net-zero goals by turning architecture into renewable energy generators.
learn.c1_text: BIPV embeds solar cells directly into building surfaces. For example, solar glass can replace traditional windows, producing up to 30% of a building's energy needs. Unlike rooftop panels, BIPV doesn’t require extra space, ideal for dense cities. This dual-purpose design cuts construction costs by merging aesthetics with function.
learn.c2_text: BIPV uses photovoltaic materials embedded in construction elements. When sunlight hits these materials, electrons are released via the photoelectric effect, generating DC electricity. Inverters then convert this to AC for home or grid use. Unlike standalone panels, BIPV’s integration ensures no visual clutter, making it popular for commercial buildings and homes.
learn.c3_text: India’s push for urban solar adoption has seen BIPV projects in cities like Bengaluru and Mumbai. The MNRE offers incentives for BIPV installations, aiming to add 10 GW by 2025. A case study in Ahmedabad shows BIPV reducing a school’s electricity bills by 40% annually. This aligns with India’s 500 GW renewable target by 2030, leveraging urban spaces efficiently.
learn.stat1: BIPV can generate 15-20% of a building’s energy needs
learn.stat2: India’s urban solar capacity could reach 30 GW by 2030
learn.stat3: BIPV installations save ~10% on construction costs
learn.stat4: Bengaluru’s BIPV pilot reduced grid dependency by 25%
